title: PLBAP Model Reproducibility Report
description: One document per model. Captures paper metadata, artifact availability, reproduction outcome, and failure analysis.

schema_version.description: Schema version this document conforms to.

literature.description: Publication metadata and reported benchmark performance.

data_linked.description: Are the training/evaluation datasets publicly linked from the paper or repo?

code_linked.description: Is the source code publicly linked?

training_set.description: Training dataset used, e.g. 'PDBbind v.2016 refined'.

casf_benchmark.description: Which CASF benchmark edition was used and what was predicted.

target.description: Raw target label as stated in the paper, e.g. 'pKd', 'pKi', 'binding affinity'.

target_unit.description: Unit as stated in the paper, e.g. 'pK units', 'kcal/mol'.

target_normalized.description: Normalized target type enabling cross-model comparison. Use 'pK_unspecified' when the paper uses 'pK' or 'binding affinity' without distinguishing Kd/Ki/Ka.

reported_scoring_power.description: CASF-2016 scoring-power metrics as reported in the paper (crystal structures).

reported_scoring_power_md.description: CASF-2016 scoring-power metrics reported for MD-ensemble inputs, if applicable.

artifacts.description: Public availability of reproducibility-critical pipeline components.

data_repo.description: Public data repository (if separate from code, e.g. Zenodo, Figshare).

availability_type.description: How the code is made available. 'github'/'gitlab' = downloadable repo; 'supplementary_only' = journal supplement only; 'web_server_only' = interactive server with no downloadable code; 'none' = no code released.

readme_description.description: One-sentence summary of what the README says the repo does.

explicit_versions.description: Dependencies are version-pinned (not just 'pip install x').

environment_file.description: A conda env YAML or requirements.txt is provided.

preprocessing_scripts.description: Scripts to go from raw PDB files to model-ready inputs are included.

inference_scripts.description: Scripts to run the pretrained model and generate predictions are included.

pretrained_weights.description: Pretrained model checkpoint(s) are publicly available.

notebook.description: A runnable Jupyter notebook demonstrating usage is provided.

example_input_data.description: Example input data (e.g. sample PDB files, processed graphs) is included in the repo.

example_intermediate_data.description: Example intermediate data (e.g. pre-computed graphs, grids) is included in the repo.

example_output_predictions.description: Example output predictions are included in the repo.

known_issues.description: Known issues or caveats documented by the authors in the README or repo.

study_fork.description: This study's fork of the upstream repo.

changes_summary.description: Free-text summary of all modifications made to achieve a runnable pipeline.

environment.description: Compute environment used for the reproduction attempt.

conda_env_file.description: Path to the env YAML file committed in the study fork.

reproducibility.description: Outcome and analysis of the reproduction attempt.

troubleshooting_time.description: Active troubleshooting time spent during the reproduction attempt. Null if no attempt was made.

barriers_to_attempt.description: Controlled-vocabulary tags describing why a reproduction attempt was not made or could not be started. Captures structural absences (e.g. no code, no weights) discovered during the audit, before any execution was attempted. Distinct from failure_modes, which captures what went wrong during an active attempt.

failure_modes.description: Controlled-vocabulary tags describing what went wrong during an active reproduction attempt. Empty array if not attempted or if fully successful.

failure_notes.description: Free-text explanation of barriers, failure modes, or unusual circumstances.

reproduced_metrics.description: Metrics actually obtained in this study. Null if not attempted or pipeline did not run.

checkpoint_used.description: Which model checkpoint was used, if multiple were available.
